 {"id":"beads_rust-c58","title":"Export Error Policies","description":"## Overview
Implement configurable error handling policies for JSONL export. Different use cases require different trade-offs between strictness and resilience.

## Error Policy Enum
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub enum ExportErrorPolicy {
    /// Abort export on any error (default for sync)
    #[default]
    Strict,
    
    /// Skip problematic records, export what we can
    BestEffort,
    
    /// Export valid records, report failures (for debugging)
    Partial,
    
    /// Only export issues (skip deps/labels/comments on error)
    RequiredCore,
}
```

## Technical Requirements

### Policy Implementation
```rust
pub struct ExportContext {
    pub policy: ExportErrorPolicy,
    pub errors: Vec<ExportError>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
}

impl ExportContext {
    pub fn handle_error(&mut self, err: ExportError) -> Result<(), ExportError> {
        match self.policy {
            ExportErrorPolicy::Strict => {
                // Fail immediately
                Err(err)
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::BestEffort => {
                // Log and continue
                tracing::warn!("Export error (skipping): {}", err);
                self.errors.push(err);
                Ok(())
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::Partial => {
                // Record for report, continue
                self.errors.push(err);
                Ok(())
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::RequiredCore => {
                // Fail only if its a core (issue) error
                match err.entity_type {
                    EntityType::Issue => Err(err),
                    _ => {
                        self.errors.push(err);
                        Ok(())
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Export With Policy
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn export_jsonl_with_policy(
        &self,
        output_dir: &Path,
        policy: ExportErrorPolicy,
    ) -> Result<ExportReport> {
        let mut ctx = ExportContext::new(policy);
        let mut report = ExportReport::new();
        
        // Export issues
        let issues = self.get_all_issues()?;
        let issues_path = output_dir.join("issues.jsonl");
        let mut issues_file = File::create(&issues_path)?;
        
        for issue in &issues {
            match serde_json::to_string(issue) {
                Ok(json) => {
                    writeln!(issues_file, "{}", json)?;
                    report.issues_exported += 1;
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    ctx.handle_error(ExportError {
                        entity_type: EntityType::Issue,
                        entity_id: issue.id.clone(),
                        message: e.to_string(),
                    })?;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Export dependencies
        let deps = self.get_all_dependencies()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        // Export labels
        let labels = self.get_all_labels()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        // Export comments
        let comments = self.get_all_comments()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        report.errors = ctx.errors;
        report.policy_used = policy;
        Ok(report)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Default: strict (fail on any error)
br sync --flush-only

# Best effort: export what we can
br sync --flush-only --error-policy best-effort

# Partial: full report of failures
br sync --flush-only --error-policy partial

# Required core: issues must succeed, others can fail
br sync --flush-only --error-policy required-core
```

### Export Report
```rust
pub struct ExportReport {
    pub issues_exported: usize,
    pub dependencies_exported: usize,
    pub labels_exported: usize,
    pub comments_exported: usize,
    pub errors: Vec<ExportError>,
    pub policy_used: ExportErrorPolicy,
}

pub struct ExportError {
    pub entity_type: EntityType,
    pub entity_id: String,
    pub message: String,
}

impl ExportReport {
    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {
        !self.errors.is_empty()
    }
    
    pub fn success_rate(&self) -> f64 {
        let total = self.issues_exported + self.dependencies_exported 
                  + self.labels_exported + self.comments_exported;
        let failed = self.errors.len();
        if total + failed == 0 { 1.0 }
        else { total as f64 / (total + failed) as f64 }
    }
}
```

### Output (Human)
```
Export completed with policy: best-effort

Exported:
  156 issues
  234 dependencies (2 errors)
  89 labels
  42 comments (1 error)

Errors (3):
  dependency bd-abc12 -> bd-xyz99: Invalid reference
  dependency bd-def34 -> bd-missing: Target not found
  comment c-123: Serialization failed
```

### Output (JSON)
```json
{
  "policy": "best-effort",
  "exported": {
    "issues": 156,
    "dependencies": 234,
    "labels": 89,
    "comments": 42
  },
  "errors": [
    { "type": "dependency", "id": "bd-abc12", "message": "Invalid reference" }
  ],
  "success_rate": 0.98
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Strict policy fails on first error
- [ ] BestEffort skips errors, continues export
- [ ] Partial exports valid records, reports all failures
- [ ] RequiredCore fails only on issue errors
- [ ] --error-policy CLI flag
- [ ] ExportReport includes error list
- [ ] Human-readable error summary
- [ ] JSON error report

## Unit Tests
- Strict policy aborts on error
- BestEffort continues after error
- Partial collects all errors
- RequiredCore fails on issue error
- RequiredCore succeeds despite dep error
- Error count accurate
- Success rate calculated correctly
- Each entity type triggers correct behavior

## Dependencies
- JSONL Export Implementation
- Model Types
- Error Handling Module

## Rationale
Different scenarios need different error tolerance. Sync should be strict to prevent silent data loss. Debugging needs partial export to identify problematic records. Backup needs best-effort to export as much as possible despite corruption.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:23:50.298979959Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:43:24.051726823Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T18:43:24.051726823Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-ciu","title":"JSONL Export Implementation","description":"# JSONL Export Implementation

## Purpose
Implement classic export semantics: atomic JSONL write, include tombstones, exclude ephemerals, safety guard against empty DB overwrites, and metadata updates.

## Export Rules
- Include **tombstones**.
- Exclude ephemerals/wisps (`ephemeral=true` or ID contains `-wisp-`).
- Sort by ID for deterministic output.
- Populate dependencies/labels/comments for each issue.

## Atomic Write
- Write to temp file in same directory → fsync → rename.
- Default permissions: 0600 (single-repo). Auto-flush may set 0644.

## Safety Guard
- Refuse to overwrite **non-empty JSONL** if DB has **zero issues** unless `--force`.

## Metadata Updates (after success)
- Clear dirty flags for exported IDs.
- Update `jsonl_content_hash` + `last_import_time`.
- Touch DB mtime to be ≥ JSONL mtime.

## Error Policies (config)
- `strict` (default), `best-effort`, `partial`, `required-core`.
- Optional `.manifest.json` with failures/warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Atomic write + deterministic ordering.
- Safety guard enforced.
- Metadata updates applied.

## Tests
- Export of empty DB with existing JSONL (guard).
- Deterministic ordering.
- Manifest output when enabled.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:32:20.260358724Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:37:02.581077346Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T16:37:02.581077346Z","close_reason":"JSONL export complete. Fixed create_issue() ephemeral/pinned/is_template bug.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-clp","title":"Scope guardrails: non-invasive boundaries + classic-only command set","description":"# Scope Guardrails (Non-Invasive + Classic-Only)

## Purpose
Lock in **non-invasive** boundaries and the **classic** command set. This bead is the scope contract for br v1.

## Required Scope (Classic v1)
- Core CRUD: `init`, `create` (incl. `--file`), `update` (incl. bulk + `--claim`), `close`, `reopen`, `delete`.
- Views: `list`, `show`, `ready`, `blocked`, `search`, `stats/status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`.
- Relations: `dep` (add/remove/list/tree/cycles), `label` (add/remove/list/list-all), `comments` (add/list).
- Scheduling: `defer`, `undefer`.
- Sync: `sync --flush-only`, `sync --import-only` (NO git ops).
- Config: YAML + DB config (`config` command), metadata.json.
- Support modes: `--no-db` JSONL-only.

Optional-but-documented (post-core):
- `where`, `info`, `version`, `q`, `lint`, `graph`, `epic`.
- Port-plan extras: saved queries, CSV export, changelog, local history backups.

## Explicit Exclusions (MUST NOT SHIP in v1)
- Daemon/RPC or background services.
- Git hooks, merge drivers, auto-commit/push/pull, sync-branch worktrees.
- Linear/Jira integrations, mail delegation.
- Gastown features (agent/molecule/gate/rig/convoy/HOP).
- Git-touching maintenance (reset/restore/repair/cleanup auto-fix).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI help reflects included commands only.
- Excluded features absent from CLI and docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:03:15.959423160Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:07.966311461Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:07.966311461Z","close_reason":"Scope guardrails acknowledged and adhered to","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-cmi","title":"CSV export format (list/export)","description":"# CSV Export Format (list/export)

## Purpose
Add CSV output for list/export for non-technical consumers (plan enhancement).

## CLI
- `br list --format=csv`
- `br export --format=csv`
- Optional `--fields` to select columns (default set below).

## Default Fields
`id,title,status,priority,issue_type,assignee,created_at,updated_at`

## Behavior
- Use a CSV writer with proper escaping.
- `--fields` can include description/notes; multi-line fields are quoted.
- Preserve deterministic ordering (same as list/export).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CSV output has header row.
- Correct field ordering and escaping.
- Works with filters (list/export).

## Tests
- CSV with commas/newlines in fields.
- Field selection.","notes":"Added E2E CSV list coverage in tests/e2e_list_priority.rs (header/escaping + --fields newline quoting). cargo test list_csv passes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CopperMeadow","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:26.967295200Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:37:40.500774613Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:37:40.500774613Z","close_reason":"CSV formatting implemented in src/format/csv.rs and list --format=csv in src/cli/commands/list.rs; E2E list CSV tests noted in issue","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
@@ -378,6 +379,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-hvf","title":"show Command Implementation","description":"# show Command

## Purpose
Display full issue details (including labels, dependencies, dependents, comments) with classic output format.

## CLI
```
br show <id...> [OPTIONS]
br show [OPTIONS]  # Uses last-touched if no ID
```

## Flags
- `<id...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution supported).
- `--short`: Compact single-line output.
- `--deps`: Include dependency details.
- `--comments`: Include comments.
- `--events`: Include event history.
- `--refs`: Include external references.
- `--json`: JSON output.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output (alias for --json).

## Behavior
1. Resolve ID(s) via partial matching.
2. Set last-touched to the first shown ID.
3. Fetch full issue details including:
   - All issue fields.
   - Labels (always included).
   - Dependencies (blocking deps).
   - Dependents (issues this blocks).
   - Comments (if `--comments` or full view).
   - Events (if `--events`).
   - Parent issue (if exists).
4. JSON output is ALWAYS an array (even for single ID).

## Output

### JSON (IssueDetails schema)
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature X",
    "description": "Full description here...",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "feature",
    "assignee": "alice",
    "created_at": "2025-01-10T09:00:00Z",
    "labels": ["backend", "api"],
    "dependencies": [
      {"depends_on_id": "bd-xyz89", "type": "blocks"}
    ],
    "dependents": [
      {"issue_id": "bd-def34", "type": "blocks"}
    ],
    "comments": [
      {"id": 1, "author": "bob", "text": "LGTM", "created_at": "2025-01-11T10:00:00Z"}
    ],
    "parent": "bd-epic1"
  }
]
```

### Text Output (Full View)
```
bd-abc12: Implement feature X
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Status: open          Priority: P1
Type: feature         Assignee: alice
Created: 2025-01-10   Owner: team-lead

Description:
  Full description here explaining what this
  feature should do and why.

Design:
  Technical design notes...

Acceptance Criteria:
  - [ ] Criterion 1
  - [ ] Criterion 2

Labels: backend, api

Dependencies (2):
  • bd-xyz89: Database schema [P0] [open]
  • bd-qrs12: API design [P1] [in_progress]

Blocking (1):
  • bd-def34: Write tests [P2] [blocked]

Comments (1):
  bob (2025-01-11):
    LGTM
```

### Short Output (`--short`)
```
bd-abc12 [P1] [open] Implement feature X (alice) [backend, api]
```

## Data Structures

### IssueDetails
```rust
pub struct IssueDetails {
    #[serde(flatten)]
    pub issue: Issue,
    pub labels: Vec<String>,
    pub dependencies: Vec<DependencyInfo>,
    pub dependents: Vec<DependencyInfo>,
    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,
    pub parent: Option<String>,
}

pub struct DependencyInfo {
    pub issue_id: String,
    pub depends_on_id: String,
    pub dep_type: String,
    pub title: Option<String>,  // For display
    pub status: Option<String>, // For display
}
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: ID does not resolve → error with suggestions.
- **AmbiguousId**: ID resolves to multiple → error with candidate list.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(ids = ?ids, "Showing issue details");
tracing::debug!(id = %id, "Resolved issue");
tracing::debug!(labels = ?labels, deps = ?deps.len(), "Fetched relations");
tracing::trace!(comments = ?comments.len(), "Loaded comments");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON array output matches bd.
- Text output matches golden snapshots.
- IssueDetails includes all relation types.
- Last-touched set correctly.

## Unit Tests (storage layer)
```rust
// tests/storage/show_tests.rs
test_get_issue_details_basic
test_get_issue_details_with_labels
test_get_issue_details_with_dependencies
test_get_issue_details_with_dependents
test_get_issue_details_with_comments
test_get_issue_details_with_parent
test_get_issue_details_not_found
test_get_issue_details_sets_last_touched
test_get_multiple_issue_details
```

## Integration Tests (CLI)
```rust
// tests/integration/show_tests.rs
#[test]
fn test_show_basic() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Show test issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Show test issue"))
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&id));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_description() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "With description", "--description", "This is the full body"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("This is the full body"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_labels() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Labeled issue", "--labels", "backend,api"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("backend"))
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("api"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_dependencies() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocking issue");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Dependent issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show blocked issue should list dependency
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &blocked])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&blocker).or(predicate::str::contains("Blocking issue")));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_dependents() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocking issue");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Dependent issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show blocker should list what it blocks
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&blocked).or(predicate::str::contains("Dependent")));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_json_output() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "JSON show test");
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json.is_array());
    assert_eq!(json[0]["title"], "JSON show test");
    assert!(json[0]["labels"].is_array());
    assert!(json[0]["dependencies"].is_array());
    assert!(json[0]["dependents"].is_array());
}

#[test]
fn test_show_multiple_ids() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id1, &id2, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json.as_array().unwrap().len(), 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_show_short_format() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Short format test", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "test"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--short"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
    // Short format should be one line
    let lines: Vec<&str> = stdout.trim().lines().collect();
    assert_eq!(lines.len(), 1);
    assert!(stdout.contains(&id));
    assert!(stdout.contains("Short format test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_not_found() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", "bd-nonexistent"])
        .assert()
        .failure()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("not found"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_partial_id() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Partial ID test", "--id", "beads_rust-abc123"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Should resolve partial ID
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", "abc123"])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Partial ID test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_last_touched() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Last touched test");
    
    // Show sets last-touched
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show without ID uses last-touched
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("show")
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Last touched test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_parent() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let parent = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Parent epic");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Child task", "--parent", &parent])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let child = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &child, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json[0]["parent"].is_string() || json[0]["dependencies"].as_array().unwrap().iter().any(|d| d["type"] == "parent-child"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_includes_all_fields() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args([
            "create", "Complete issue",
            "--description", "Full description",
            "--design", "Design notes",
            "--acceptance", "Acceptance criteria",
            "--notes", "Additional notes",
            "--priority", "1",
            "--type", "feature",
            "--assignee", "alice",
            "--labels", "backend,api"
        ])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    let issue = &json[0];
    
    assert_eq!(issue["title"], "Complete issue");
    assert_eq!(issue["description"], "Full description");
    assert_eq!(issue["design"], "Design notes");
    assert_eq!(issue["acceptance_criteria"], "Acceptance criteria");
    assert_eq!(issue["notes"], "Additional notes");
    assert_eq!(issue["priority"], 1);
    assert_eq!(issue["issue_type"], "feature");
    assert_eq!(issue["assignee"], "alice");
}
```

- Body: normal, word-wrapped
- Separator: dim horizontal line

fn test_comments_list_shows_all() {

    for comment in &comments {
        assert!(output.contains(&comment.author));
        assert!(output.contains(&comment.body));

    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_comment(&comment, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("@alice") || output.contains("alice"));
}

#[test]
fn test_comments_shows_timestamp() {
    let comment = Comment { created_at: recent_time, .. };
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_comment(&comment, &ctx);
    // Should show relative time
    assert!(output.contains("ago") || output.contains("just now"));
}

    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_comments_list(&comments, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("No comments") || output.is_empty());
}

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Comments Command ==="

# Setup
setup_test_db
TEST_ID=$(br create "Comments test issue" --silent 2>&1)

# Test 1: Add comment
log_step "Testing comment add"
ADD_OUTPUT=$(br comments add "$TEST_ID" "This is a test comment" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$ADD_OUTPUT" "Added" "Should confirm comment added"
log_pass "comment add works"

# Test 2: List comments
log_step "Testing comment list"
LIST_OUTPUT=$(br comments list "$TEST_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$LIST_OUTPUT" "test comment" "Should show comment"
log_pass "comment list works"

# Test 3: Multiple comments
log_step "Testing multiple comments"
br comments add "$TEST_ID" "Second comment" 2>&1 > /dev/null
br comments add "$TEST_ID" "Third comment" 2>&1 > /dev/null
MULTI_OUTPUT=$(br comments list "$TEST_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$MULTI_OUTPUT" "Second comment"
assert_contains "$MULTI_OUTPUT" "Third comment"
log_pass "Multiple comments work"

# Test 4: JSON output
log_step "Testing JSON output"
JSON_OUTPUT=$(br comments list "$TEST_ID" --json 2>&1)
COUNT=$(echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | jq '. | length')
assert_eq "$COUNT" "3" "Should have 3 comments"
log_pass "JSON output works"

# Test 5: Rich mode
log_step "Testing rich mode"
RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br comments list "$TEST_ID" 2>&1 || true)
log_pass "Rich mode renders"

log_success "=== Comments command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="

 {"id":"beads_rust-i7s","title":"where Command Implementation","description":"# where Command Implementation

## Purpose
Report the active `.beads` directory location, including redirects and prefix detection.

## Behavior
- Find active beads dir (respect `BEADS_DIR` if set).
- If `.beads/redirect` exists, follow it and report `redirected_from`.
- Detect prefix:
  - Prefer DB config `issue_prefix` if DB available.
  - Else parse prefix from first JSONL line ID.
- Error when no beads dir:
  - Text: error + hint, exit 1.
  - JSON: `{ "error": "no beads directory found" }`, exit 1.

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "path": "/abs/path/.beads",
  "redirected_from": "/abs/other/.beads",
  "prefix": "bd",
  "database_path": "/abs/path/.beads/beads.db"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct path resolution with and without redirect.
- Prefix detection matches classic order.
- Error behavior matches bd.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyOwl","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:32.897767057Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:00:03.997180494Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:00:03.997180494Z","close_reason":"Implemented where command with text and JSON output modes. ### Traffic Level: LOW
Version and update management.

### br version
```
╭─ br version ────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  br 0.1.7                                           │
│                                                     │
│  Build Info:                                        │
│  ├── Commit    abc123d                              │
│  ├── Built     2024-01-15                           │
│  ├── Rust      1.85.0                               │
│  └── Target    x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu             │
│                                                     │
│  Features: self_update                              │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### br upgrade [--check]
Check for updates:
```
╭─ Upgrade Check ─────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Current version:  0.1.7                            │
│  Latest version:   0.1.9                            │
│                                                     │
│  Changes in 0.1.9:                                  │
│  - Added rich terminal output                       │
│  - Fixed sync race condition                        │
│  - Improved performance                             │
│                                                     │
│  Run 'br upgrade' to install                        │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

Performing upgrade:
```
Downloading br 0.1.9...
████████████████████ 100%

✓ Upgraded br from 0.1.7 to 0.1.9
```

### Version Comparison
Highlight version difference:
- Green: upgrade available
- Yellow: pre-release
- Current: normal

### Testing Requirements

#### Unit Tests
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_version_display_formatting() {
        let info = VersionInfo {
            version: "0.1.7".to_string(),
            commit: Some("abc123d".to_string()),
            build_date: Some("2024-01-15".to_string()),
            rust_version: Some("1.85.0".to_string()),
            target: Some("x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu".to_string()),
            features: vec!["self_update".to_string()],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_version(&info, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("0.1.7"));
        assert!(output.contains("abc123d"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_version_minimal_info() {
        let info = VersionInfo {
            version: "0.1.7".to_string(),
            commit: None,
            build_date: None,
            rust_version: None,
            target: None,
            features: vec![],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_version(&info, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("0.1.7"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_upgrade_check_formatting() {
        let check = UpgradeCheck {
            current: "0.1.7".to_string(),
            latest: "0.1.9".to_string(),
            changelog: vec![
                "Added rich output".to_string(),
                "Fixed sync issue".to_string(),
            ],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_upgrade_check(&check, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("0.1.7"));
        assert!(output.contains("0.1.9"));
        assert!(output.contains("rich output"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_upgrade_check_up_to_date() {
        let check = UpgradeCheck {
            current: "0.1.9".to_string(),
            latest: "0.1.9".to_string(),
            changelog: vec![],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_upgrade_check(&check, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("up to date") || output.contains("0.1.9"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_json_mode_version() {
        let info = VersionInfo {
            version: "0.1.7".to_string(),
            commit: Some("abc123d".to_string()),
            build_date: None,
            rust_version: None,
            target: None,
            features: vec![],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_version(&info, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(parsed["version"], "0.1.7");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_progress_bar_rendering() {
        let progress = DownloadProgress {
            downloaded: 50,
            total: 100,
            percentage: 50.0,
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_download_progress(&progress, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("50%") || output.contains("█"));
    }
}
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Version/Upgrade Commands ==="

log_step "Testing br version"
VERSION_OUTPUT=$(br version)
log_debug "Version output: $VERSION_OUTPUT"
if echo "$VERSION_OUTPUT" | grep -qE "br [0-9]+\.[0-9]+"; then
    log_pass "Version displayed correctly"
else
    log_fail "Version not displayed"
    exit 1
fi

log_step "Testing br version --json"
VERSION_JSON=$(br version --json)
log_debug "JSON: $VERSION_JSON"
echo "$VERSION_JSON" | jq -e '.version'
log_pass "Version JSON valid"

# Note: upgrade --check may fail if no network or release endpoint
log_step "Testing br upgrade --check (may skip if offline)"
UPGRADE_CHECK=$(br upgrade --check 2>&1 || true)
log_debug "Upgrade check: $UPGRADE_CHECK"
if echo "$UPGRADE_CHECK" | grep -qi "version\|error\|offline"; then
    log_pass "Upgrade check executed"
else
    log_warn "Upgrade check inconclusive"
fi

log_pass "=== All version/upgrade tests passed ==="
```
